findings.evidence: `rg -n "oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four" code/TauCeti/NumberTheory/Multiquadratic/PrimeDiscriminant.lean` shows line 96 and line 108 are plain `theorem`s, while the roadmap needs `p*` radicands satisfying `p* ≡ 1 (mod 4)` at `roadmap/TauCetiRoadmap/Multiquadratic/README.md:122`.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to `oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four` and, if the prime wrapper is kept public, to `oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four_of_prime` as well.
findings.issue: `oddPrimeDiscriminant_emod_four` is the characteristic normal-form lemma for the new radicand, but it is not marked `@[simp]`; the prime-specialized wrapper at line 108 has the same issue if users carry `p.Prime` and `p ≠ 2` rather than `Odd p`.

summary: The exposed surface is mostly scoped to the odd-prime discriminant target, but its central mod-four normal form is not available to automation. Downstream genus-field code should be able to simplify the radicand congruence without naming the lemma manually.
